topic_groups.topic: Public Health, Policy and Care Delivery

articles.title: Down syndrome isn't a tragedy, but misinformation about it is
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article argues that medical and social misinformation about Down syndrome shapes prenatal counseling, family decisions, and public assumptions. It emphasizes the consequences of deficit-focused framing, including institutionalization and exclusion.

articles.title: As patients move through injury care, well-intentioned changes can backfire
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers argue that injury-care improvements can fail or cause unintended effects if the broader health system is not prepared. The findings emphasize system-wide coordination rather than isolated reforms.

articles.title: Smoking restrictions gain support across all 50 states over 30 years
articles.summary_3_sentences: A UC San Diego study developed and validated a way to measure changing U.S. social norms around smoking restrictions and secondhand smoke. The findings suggest support for restrictions has increased across all 50 states over three decades.

articles.title: Most people seeking a kidney transplant in the US never reach the waitlist
articles.summary_3_sentences: A study found that many U.S. patients with kidney failure who are referred for transplant never start or complete the evaluation needed for waitlisting. The report highlights barriers before patients reach the formal transplant list.

articles.title: The over‑50s are most likely to overdose. Here's how older people use drugs
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article discusses overdose patterns among older Australians, noting that overdose deaths are increasingly affecting people over 50. It challenges assumptions that overdose risk is concentrated mainly among younger people.

articles.title: Survey reveals major gaps in cardio-oncology training
articles.summary_3_sentences: An international survey presented at ESC Cardio-Oncology 2026 found major gaps in cardio-oncology training. The findings point to a need for more structured education in this expanding clinical field.

articles.title: What drives women to have a 'freebirth' without a midwife or doctor? Here's what the research says
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article reviews research on why some women choose freebirth without a doctor or midwife. It is framed around a coronial inquest after a home freebirth death in Melbourne.

articles.title: Why grandparents matter more than ever for children's mental health
articles.summary_3_sentences: A child psychologist argues that grandparents can support youth mental health through listening, positive experiences, and meaningful connection. The article emphasizes emotional resilience rather than achievement pressure.

articles.title: A COVID-19 Preventive Strategy is Now FDA Approved
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article reports an FDA-approved preventive strategy for COVID-19. It presents the development in the context of ongoing efforts to prevent and treat COVID-19.

articles.title: National Cancer Survivors Day Celebrates Survivorship
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article marks National Cancer Survivors Day as a celebration of people with a history of cancer. It focuses on survivorship recognition rather than a specific clinical study.

articles.title: The supplements older adults actually need and the ones they don't
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article reviews supplement use in older adults, emphasizing that benefit depends on specific deficiencies or nutritional needs. It highlights B12, vitamin D, and protein while cautioning that excess supplements can cause harm or interact with medications.

articles.title: Single amino acid change may help viruses jump from bat to human
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article reports research suggesting that a single amino acid change may influence whether bat viruses can infect humans. It is framed around zoonotic spillover and pandemic origins.

articles.title: Ancient DNA reveals plague was already killing humans 5,500 years ago
articles.summary_3_sentences: Ancient DNA analysis from Siberian hunter-gatherer cemeteries found early plague strains in many individuals. The findings suggest family-based outbreaks caused deaths long before historic urban plague conditions.

articles.title: New study explores potential cross-species spread of chronic wasting disease
articles.summary_3_sentences: A study found that chronic wasting disease prions may be present in animals without symptoms. The article notes no confirmed human risk but says cross-species spread and disease evolution warrant monitoring.

articles.title: New fentanyl vaccine blocks deadly overdoses before they start
articles.summary_3_sentences: An experimental Scripps Research vaccine is designed to prevent fentanyl and related designer drugs from reaching the brain. The approach trains the immune system to recognize a broader class of fentanyl-related compounds.

articles.title: The deadly tapeworm spreading across America has reached the Pacific Northwest
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers detected Echinococcus multilocularis in coyotes around Puget Sound, marking its first reported presence in wild animals on the U.S. West Coast. The parasite can cause severe disease in humans, though the article focuses on animal surveillance.

articles.title: AI-designed universal coronavirus vaccine passes first human trial
articles.summary_3_sentences: An AI-designed universal coronavirus vaccine was reported safe and well tolerated in a first human trial. It generated immune responses against SARS-CoV-2, SARS, and related bat coronaviruses.

articles.title: Outbreak Updates: Measles in the US, Ebola in the DRC
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article reports updates on measles in the United States and Ebola in the Democratic Republic of the Congo. It cites 2,288 confirmed measles cases and highlights ongoing public health concerns.

articles.title: Revealing the Secrets of a Bacterial Enzyme in a Quest for New Antibiotics
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article describes research into a bacterial enzyme that may inform antibiotic development. It highlights structural or functional features that could be exploited for new antimicrobial strategies.

articles.title: Tubulin prevents toxic brain protein clumps linked to Alzheimer's and Parkinson's
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers found that tubulin may redirect tau and alpha-synuclein away from toxic clumping inside brain cells. The work points to a possible mechanism relevant to Alzheimer's and Parkinson's disease.

articles.title: Yale study finds nearly half of older adults improved with age
articles.summary_3_sentences: A long-term Yale study found that nearly half of adults over 65 improved physically, mentally, or both over time. More positive views of aging were associated with greater likelihood of improvement.

articles.title: Scientists discover neurons must break their DNA to build the brain
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers found that migrating newborn neurons in the developing brain can experience double-strand DNA breaks while squeezing through tight spaces. The young brain appears to repair this damage quickly during development.

articles.title: Scientists reprogram brain immune cells to fight Alzheimer's
articles.summary_3_sentences: A molecule called OLE restored brain immune cells to a more protective state in Alzheimer's models. The treatment reduced plaque buildup and improved memory in preclinical experiments.

articles.title: Scientists May Have Found What Really Triggers Alzheimer's Disease
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article describes research suggesting amyloid beta may harm neurons by interfering with tau function. The finding reframes plaques as part of a broader molecular process rather than a sole trigger.

articles.title: 10 surprising ways diabetes and dementia are connected
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article reviews links between diabetes and dementia involving insulin, glucose metabolism, inflammation, and blood vessel damage. It also notes research into whether some diabetes medications may affect dementia risk.

articles.title: Copper drug clears toxic Alzheimer's proteins and restores memory
articles.summary_3_sentences: A copper-based compound improved clearance of toxic Alzheimer's-related proteins in laboratory experiments. The article notes that the compound has previously been tested in humans for other neurological conditions.

articles.title: Your brain can keep improving into your 90s, study finds
articles.summary_3_sentences: A three-year study of nearly 4,000 adults found measurable brain-health gains across ages 19 to 94 after brief daily brain-training activities. The findings challenge assumptions that cognitive function must decline with age.

articles.title: Learning a musical instrument in your 70s could help protect memory
articles.summary_3_sentences: A four-year study found that older adults who continued practicing a musical instrument maintained memory performance and showed less age-related brain shrinkage than those who quit. The reported benefits involved brain areas linked to memory and learning.

articles.title: A hidden gene finally explains this rare neurological disorder
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers identified CD99L2 mutations as a possible cause of a rare movement disorder after analyzing nearly 3,000 patients. The gene appears to help maintain nerve-cell communication.

articles.title: Scientists discover a surprising cancer link to Alzheimer's disease
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers reported that mutations linked to blood cancers may contribute to Alzheimer's disease by creating overly inflammatory brain immune cells. The article suggests possible implications for screening and treatment research.

articles.title: Scientists discover a hidden cause of aging cells that can be reversed
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers found that declining phosphatidylcholine may contribute to age-related mitochondrial dysfunction. Boosting the nutrient restored more youthful mitochondrial function in aging organisms.

articles.title: Scientists mapped every neural connection in a fruit fly and found a surprise
articles.summary_3_sentences: A complete connectome of the adult fruit fly central nervous system mapped neural connections across brain and body circuits. The findings suggest complex behavior emerges from distributed local circuits.

articles.title: Popular joint supplement glucosamine linked to faster Alzheimer's progression
articles.summary_3_sentences: A study linked glucosamine use with faster progression from mild cognitive impairment to Alzheimer's disease. The report describes a 25% higher likelihood of dementia among users and possible biological explanations.

articles.title: That ringing in your ears could be an early warning sign of hearing loss
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article explains that tinnitus and difficulty following speech in noise can be early signs of hearing loss. It notes possible effects on sleep, mood, concentration, falls, and cognitive decline.

articles.title: Scientists found a new Alzheimer's trigger and a drug that stops it
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers identified a new Alzheimer's target and created an experimental compound that blocks a damaging cellular process. In mice, the treatment slowed nerve-cell loss and reduced Alzheimer's-related changes.

articles.title: CBD Shows Early Promise for Alzheimer's
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article reports that cannabidiol reduced brain inflammation in a mouse model of Alzheimer's disease. The finding is preclinical and does not establish clinical benefit in humans.

articles.title: Scientists found the hidden switch fueling alzheimer's brain inflammation
articles.summary_3_sentences: Scripps Research scientists identified a chemically altered form of STING that may keep brain immune activity overactive in Alzheimer's disease. The work focuses on a molecular mechanism of neuroinflammation.

articles.title: Caffeine reversed memory problems caused by sleep deprivation
articles.summary_3_sentences: In laboratory studies, sleep deprivation impaired a brain circuit involved in social memory. Caffeine restored communication in that pathway and reversed the memory deficit without broadly overstimulating normal function.

articles.title: Protein traffic jams may explain aging, memory loss, and Alzheimer's
articles.summary_3_sentences: Stanford researchers studying turquoise killifish found that aging ribosomes can stall and collide while building proteins. The resulting faulty proteins and clumps may relate to aging, memory loss, and Alzheimer's disease.

articles.title: CBD may slow Alzheimer's by calming the brain's immune system
articles.summary_3_sentences: In Alzheimer's mouse experiments, inhaled CBD reduced drivers of neuroinflammation linked to memory loss and degeneration. The findings are preclinical and do not establish human efficacy.

articles.title: Human organoids reveal how to reverse "irreversible" nerve damage
articles.summary_3_sentences: Cambridge researchers used brain-and-spinal-cord organoids to study why human neurons lose regrowth ability during development. They identified a gene network and an existing hormone drug that boosted nerve fiber regrowth in the model.

articles.title: Researchers block key protein that helps Parkinson's spread through the brain
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers identified GPNMB as a protein that may help Parkinson's pathology spread between cells. Early antibody experiments blocked this toxic process in preclinical work.

articles.title: Scientists thought brain inflammation was driving long COVID but the scans told a different story
articles.summary_3_sentences: A brain imaging study found no evidence of widespread brain inflammation in patients with prolonged symptoms after COVID-19. More severe symptoms were associated with increased activity in mood- and emotion-related brain regions.

articles.title: Scientists create supercharged vitamin K that helps the brain heal itself
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers in Japan created vitamin K-based compounds that were more effective than natural vitamin K at turning neural stem cells into neurons. The work is early-stage and aimed at future neuroregeneration research.

articles.title: USC scientists discover a hidden Alzheimer's trigger and a possible way to shut it down
articles.summary_3_sentences: USC researchers identified cPLA2 as a possible driver of harmful brain inflammation in Alzheimer's disease, especially in APOE4-related risk. They also described potential compounds that target the enzyme.

articles.title: Clues to psilocybin's epigenetic effects on people with alcohol use disorder
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article describes research into possible epigenetic effects of psilocybin in people with alcohol use disorder. It frames psychedelics as an emerging area of mental health treatment research.

articles.title: How dopamine modulates brain circuits of motivation in a behavioral addiction model
articles.summary_3_sentences: Kanazawa University researchers identified dopamine-related neural mechanisms of motivation in a mouse model of behavioral addiction. The work is preclinical and may inform future treatment research.

articles.title: New study attempts to reconcile conflicting findings on benefits vs. risks of alcohol consumption
articles.summary_3_sentences: A review examined conflicting findings about alcohol consumption and health. It reports that many diseases are fully attributable to alcohol while also noting that some harms can be slowed or reversed by reducing or quitting drinking.

articles.title: Three genes may link six mental disorders through shared biomarkers
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article reports research suggesting three genes may act as shared biomarkers across six neuropsychiatric or neurodevelopmental conditions. The work focuses on common biological markers rather than disorder-specific mechanisms.

articles.title: How people use music shapes their emotional experiences, new study finds
articles.summary_3_sentences: A University of Jyväskylä study examined who is more likely to experience mixed emotions while listening to music. The findings suggest music-related emotional experiences vary by how people use and relate to music.

articles.title: Nordic walking significantly reduces depression symptoms in as little as five weeks, trial finds
articles.summary_3_sentences: A trial found that Nordic walking reduced depression symptoms within five weeks. The article describes the activity as a full-body aerobic exercise using poles.

articles.title: Dementia care: Re‑envisioning the role of music
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article discusses music in dementia care, drawing from clinical and research experience in music therapy. It cautions against viewing music only as a low-cost symptom treatment and emphasizes meaning in care.

articles.title: 64% of Cannabis Consumers Say It Helps Them Sleep
articles.summary_3_sentences: A survey from the American Academy of Sleep Medicine found that 64% of cannabis consumers reported improved sleep. The article discusses possible mechanisms but is based on reported experience.

articles.title: Ozempic and Wegovy linked to surprising drop in violent behavior
articles.summary_3_sentences: A Rutgers study linked GLP-1 drug use with a weaker association between impulsive tendencies and violent behavior. Researchers stressed that causality has not been proven.

articles.title: A daily probiotic may help relieve depression and anxiety
articles.summary_3_sentences: A small clinical trial found that older adults with depression who took a daily probiotic alongside antidepressants had slightly greater symptom improvement than placebo recipients. The findings are preliminary.

articles.title: Scientists found an early depression clue hidden in children's eyes
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers found that depression and family history may affect how children attend to emotional facial expressions. Higher inherited risk was linked to greater attention to sadness.

articles.title: Higher Tyrosine in Men Linked to Shorter Lifespan
articles.summary_3_sentences: A population analysis linked higher tyrosine levels with shorter lifespan in men. The article suggests caution around tyrosine supplementation but does not establish causality.

articles.title: This popular brain supplement was linked to shorter lifespans in men
articles.summary_3_sentences: A large-scale study linked higher levels of tyrosine with shorter lifespans in men. The article notes tyrosine is commonly marketed for focus and performance, but the association needs careful interpretation.

articles.title: Your brain was never designed for this much bad news
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article discusses how human attention to danger may be overwhelmed by constant exposure to negative news. Researchers recommend healthier habits around news consumption rather than avoidance.

articles.title: Why middle age is becoming a breaking point in the U.S.
articles.summary_3_sentences: An international study found worse loneliness, depression, memory, and health among middle-aged Americans than earlier generations. The article cites financial strain, weaker social supports, and chronic stress as possible contributors.

articles.title: World's largest opioid review finds they often don't work
articles.summary_3_sentences: A large review found opioids often provide only small, short-lived benefits for acute pain and may perform no better than placebo for some conditions. The review also reported more side effects and warned that dependence can begin after short use.

articles.title: Popular GLP-1 weight-loss drugs linked to lower risks of addiction and overdose
articles.summary_3_sentences: A study of more than 600,000 U.S. veterans linked GLP-1 medication use with lower risks of substance use disorders and fewer overdose-related outcomes. The findings are observational and do not prove causality.

articles.title: Scientists reverse anxiety by fixing a tiny brain circuit
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers identified amygdala neurons involved in anxiety and social behavior. Restoring activity in this circuit reversed anxiety and social deficits in mice.

articles.title: Arthritis Drug Shows Promise for Depression
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article reports that tocilizumab, an anti-inflammatory drug used for rheumatoid arthritis, showed promise for moderate-to-severe depression. The summary indicates an immune-targeted approach to depression treatment.

articles.title: New depression treatment targets the immune system instead of the brain
articles.summary_3_sentences: A small clinical trial found that an anti-inflammatory drug used for rheumatoid arthritis appeared to reduce symptoms in hard-to-treat depression. The study also reported improvements in fatigue, anxiety, and quality of life.

articles.title: Only a third of eligible breast cancer patients receive a gene expression test
articles.summary_3_sentences: A Netherlands study found that only about one-third of eligible breast cancer patients receive a gene expression test that can help avoid unnecessary chemotherapy. The low uptake persisted even after reimbursement.

articles.title: Mass spectrometry platform could shrink cancer drug discovery cycle to four hours
articles.summary_3_sentences: Purdue researchers developed a mass spectrometry platform intended to speed identification of promising cancer drug compounds. The technology targets a slow step in early drug discovery.

articles.title: Major review finds vaping likely causes lung and oral cancer
articles.summary_3_sentences: A review concluded that nicotine vaping is likely to cause lung and oral cancers based on human biomarkers, animal studies, and laboratory evidence. The findings challenge claims that vaping is harmless.

articles.title: This DNA repair gene went rogue and exposed a cancer weakness
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers found that overproduction of the DNA repair gene EXO1 can cause inappropriate DNA cutting and damage linked to cancer. The finding may expose a vulnerability in cancer cells.

articles.title: Combination Therapy Shows Promise for Hormone-Receptor Positive Breast Cancer
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article reports on a combination therapy showing promise for hormone-receptor-positive breast cancer. It provides limited details but frames the work against the high prevalence of breast cancer.

articles.title: Scientists shut down cancer DNA repair to overcome drug resistance
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers found that UNI418 can disrupt DNA repair in cancer cells and restore response to PARP inhibition in resistant cells. The work suggests a strategy for overcoming drug resistance.

articles.title: GLP-1 Drugs Linked to 35% Lower Risk of Breast Cancer
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article reports an association between GLP-1 drug use and lower breast cancer risk. The summary indicates an observational link and does not prove prevention.

articles.title: Cancer patients found a simple way to stay mentally sharp during chemotherapy
articles.summary_3_sentences: In a trial, cancer patients following a home-based exercise program had better attention and fewer perceived cognitive problems during chemotherapy than placebo controls. Low-dose ibuprofen showed less consistent cognitive effects.

articles.title: The secret reason some cancer treatments stop working
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers identified SLAMF6 as an immune checkpoint-like brake that can weaken cancer-fighting T cells. Antibodies blocking SLAMF6 improved anti-tumor activity in mice.

articles.title: New Insights Into the Link Between Colon Cancer and a Bacterial Toxin
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article examines links between colon cancer and a toxin associated with gut bacteria such as Bacteroides fragilis. It emphasizes the need for further research into microbial contributions to cancer.

articles.title: Combination Chemotherapy and Immunotherapy Treatment for Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article discusses combination chemotherapy and immunotherapy for non-small cell lung cancer. Details are limited, but it frames the approach within NSCLC treatment.

articles.title: Ozempic and similar weight-loss drugs linked to 30% lower breast cancer risk
articles.summary_3_sentences: A large study found that women taking GLP-1 drugs were about 30% less likely to develop breast cancer. Researchers said the association is promising but not proof, and clinical trials are planned.

articles.title: Scientists finally crack an "undruggable" pancreatic cancer target and nearly double survival
articles.summary_3_sentences: A clinical trial of daraxonrasib, a drug targeting KRAS-driven pancreatic cancer, reportedly nearly doubled survival in advanced disease. The article describes KRAS as a historically difficult cancer target.

articles.title: Why cancer spreads more in middle age than in old age
articles.summary_3_sentences: In mouse melanoma research, cancer spread was lowest in young mice, highest in middle-aged mice, and lower again in very old mice. The study points to immune cells that may keep cancer dormant.

articles.title: Scientists discover why some DNA-doubled cells refuse to die
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers studied cells that copy DNA but fail to divide, leaving doubled genetic material. The work may be relevant to aging, cancer, and other diseases linked to abnormal cell division.

topic_groups.topic: Metabolic Health, GLP-1 Therapies and Diabetes

articles.title: Ozempic changed obesity treatment, but experts say the real revolution is next
articles.summary_3_sentences: Experts describe GLP-1 drugs as reshaping obesity treatment and anticipate more integrated care using medications, procedures, surgery, and precision medicine. The article is a forward-looking overview rather than a single trial.

articles.title: A surprising discovery reveals the kidney has a secret backup system
articles.summary_3_sentences: Mayo Clinic researchers found a previously unrecognized water-conservation system in the kidneys that works independently of a hormone long considered central. The finding emerged during probenecid experiments in polycystic kidney disease research.

articles.title: Semaglutide Linked to Lower Risk of Bone Fracture
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article reports that semaglutide may be associated with lower fracture risk among people with type 2 diabetes. Details are limited, and the wording indicates an association.

articles.title: Most people who stop GLP-1 drugs like Ozempic eventually return
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers found that many people with type 2 diabetes who stop GLP-1 therapies later restart treatment. Newer medications appeared to support longer persistence, while side effects remained a common reason for stopping.

articles.title: Semaglutide (Ozempic) linked to fewer bone fractures despite greater weight loss
articles.summary_3_sentences: A real-world study of nearly 60,000 adults with type 2 diabetes linked semaglutide use with about 15% fewer fractures than other common weight-loss medications. The association occurred despite greater weight loss.

articles.title: New GLP-1 diabetes pill delivers major weight loss and blood sugar control
articles.summary_3_sentences: A new oral GLP-1 medication improved blood sugar control and weight loss in a major clinical trial of people with type 2 diabetes. The article highlights the convenience of a pill formulation.

articles.title: People taking GLP-1 weight loss drugs like Ozempic started moving less
articles.summary_3_sentences: Fitbit data analysis found that people taking GLP-1 weight-loss drugs reduced daily steps and exercise levels after starting medication. The article notes concerns about preserving muscle and long-term health.

articles.title: Reversing prediabetes cuts risk of deadly heart problems by 58%
articles.summary_3_sentences: Long-term studies in the U.S. and China linked reversal of prediabetes with lower risks of cardiovascular death, heart failure hospitalization, heart attack, and stroke. The reported benefits lasted for decades.

articles.title: Scientists discover why ozempic may not work for some people
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers identified genetic variants that may reduce response to GLP-1 drugs in type 2 diabetes. The variants may affect about 10% of people and were linked to lower likelihood of reaching blood sugar targets.

articles.title: This new diabetes pill burns fat without the downsides of Ozempic
articles.summary_3_sentences: An experimental diabetes and obesity pill activates skeletal muscle metabolism rather than suppressing appetite. Early clinical results suggest it lowered blood sugar, increased fat burning, preserved muscle mass, and was well tolerated.

articles.title: Omega-3 fish oil shows promise against type 2 diabetes
articles.summary_3_sentences: In diabetic rats, omega-3 supplementation improved blood sugar, cholesterol, and inflammation. The findings are preclinical and do not establish the same effects in humans.

articles.title: Scientists discover why Ozempic and Wegovy weight loss eventually plateaus
articles.summary_3_sentences: NIH research found that semaglutide triggers different responses in appetite-controlling brain cells, potentially explaining variable effects and weight-loss plateaus. The study also suggests a possible way to extend drug effects.

articles.title: AI scans 400,000 Reddit posts and finds hidden Ozempic side effects
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers used AI to analyze more than 400,000 Reddit posts about GLP-1 drugs and found discussions of symptoms such as menstrual irregularities, chills, and hot flashes. The study suggests social media analysis may help detect side effects missed in trials.

articles.title: Wegovy linked to rare "eye stroke" that can cause sudden blindness
articles.summary_3_sentences: An analysis of FDA side-effect reports found a signal linking Wegovy to a rare eye stroke associated with sudden vision loss. The article reports a stronger signal for Wegovy than Ozempic but does not establish causality.

articles.title: Researchers found 8 common food additives linked to high blood pressure and heart disease
articles.summary_3_sentences: A study of more than 112,000 people linked high intake of certain preservatives with higher risks of hypertension and cardiovascular disease. Participants were followed for up to eight years.

articles.title: Sleep apnea's hidden heart disease trigger found in the gut
articles.summary_3_sentences: In mice, disabling the bile acid receptor FXR reduced plaque buildup linked to sleep apnea-associated cardiovascular risk. The study points to gut microbial signaling as a possible treatment target.

articles.title: New drug could finally stop deadly fatty liver disease
articles.summary_3_sentences: UC San Diego researchers reported clinical trial improvements with ION224, an experimental drug for MASH that targets a liver enzyme involved in fat buildup and inflammation. Patients showed liver-health improvements without weight loss.

articles.title: Beet juice lowers blood pressure in older adults in just 2 weeks
articles.summary_3_sentences: A study found that nitrate-rich beetroot juice changed oral bacteria and reduced blood pressure in older adults after two weeks. The effect was not reported in younger adults.

articles.title: Common heart drug taken by millions found useless -- and possibly dangerous
articles.summary_3_sentences: A large international study found beta blockers offered no clear benefit after uncomplicated heart attacks in patients with normal heart function. Women taking the drugs had higher risk of adverse outcomes in the study.

articles.title: Common dizziness drug class shows promise for treating vestibular migraine
articles.summary_3_sentences: A study found that gepants, given orally or intranasally, may be effective and well tolerated for vestibular migraine. The findings were published in Otolaryngology-Head and Neck Surgery.

articles.title: Neuroimmune abnormalities may play a key role in fibromyalgia
articles.summary_3_sentences: A University of Barcelona study suggests central nervous system neuroimmune alterations may contribute to fibromyalgia. The article frames the work as a step toward understanding mechanisms behind chronic widespread pain and fatigue.

articles.title: Scientists found a cannabis compound that relieves pain without the high
articles.summary_3_sentences: Cannabis-derived terpenes reduced pain in mouse models of fibromyalgia and post-surgical pain, with geraniol showing strong effects. The compounds are not associated with THC-like psychoactive effects, but the evidence is preclinical.

articles.title: Inflammation Worsens Brain Damage
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article discusses how inflammation may worsen outcomes after traumatic brain injury. It emphasizes managing inflammation as a possible route to better recovery.

articles.title: New procedure delivers lasting knee arthritis pain relief without surgery
articles.summary_3_sentences: A minimally invasive knee osteoarthritis procedure blocking inflammation-driving blood vessels provided pain relief and improved function lasting at least a year. The article presents it as a possible option before knee replacement.

articles.title: Calcium and Vitamin D Don't Prevent Bone Fractures
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article reports that calcium and vitamin D supplements, alone or together, do not protect older adults from fractures or falls. Details are limited but align with a large review topic.

articles.title: Millions take calcium and vitamin D for stronger bones. A major review finds little benefit
articles.summary_3_sentences: A review of nearly 154,000 people found calcium, vitamin D, or both provided little to no meaningful protection against fractures or falls for most older adults. The article challenges routine supplement use for bone protection without clear indication.

articles.title: Stanford scientists regrow lost cartilage and reverse arthritis in major breakthrough
articles.summary_3_sentences: A treatment blocking an aging-related protein restored cartilage in old mice and helped prevent arthritis after knee injury. Human cartilage samples showed similar regeneration signals, but the main evidence is preclinical.

articles.title: Drug Delays Arthritis by up to 4 Years in High-Risk Patients
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article reports that abatacept delayed rheumatoid arthritis onset for up to four years in high-risk patients. It describes a preventive or disease-delaying approach.

articles.title: This drug delayed rheumatoid arthritis for years after treatment ended
articles.summary_3_sentences: A study found that one year of abatacept delayed rheumatoid arthritis onset by up to four years in high-risk people. The reported benefits persisted after treatment ended.

articles.title: Scientists "recharge" damaged nerves to ease chronic pain
articles.summary_3_sentences: Duke researchers found that supplying damaged nerves with healthy mitochondria may restore nerve function and reduce chronic nerve pain. The work proposes a source-directed mechanism for pain relief.

articles.title: Frozen fruit and canned veg are cheap, but are they as healthy as fresh food?
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article discusses whether frozen fruit and canned vegetables can be healthy alternatives to fresh produce. It is framed around low fruit and vegetable intake and affordability.

articles.title: Think you're eating healthy? You may be missing this heart-protecting nutrient
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers highlighted flavanol-rich foods such as berries, plums, apples, broad beans, cherries, and green tea as contributors to heart health. The article says five servings of fruits and vegetables may not ensure adequate flavanol intake.

articles.title: Scientists say most of what's in your food is still a mystery
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article describes "nutritional dark matter," thousands of food chemicals not captured by standard nutrition labels. Researchers suggest these compounds may help explain diet-related differences in disease risk and aging.

articles.title: Scientists turned red lettuce green and something surprising happened
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers used genome editing to block red pigment production in lettuce, causing other beneficial plant compounds to accumulate. The lettuce grew normally, suggesting a route to crops with customized nutrition profiles.

articles.title: The secret behind smoother, better-tasting protein shakes
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers developed a whey protein manufacturing method that reduces bitterness while preserving texture. They traced the unwanted flavor to concentrated minerals and found a way to remove them.

articles.title: Scientists found a surprising problem with sugar-free diets
articles.summary_3_sentences: In mice, a sucrose-free low-fat diet worsened blood sugar control, inflammation, gut bacteria, and signs of fatty liver compared with a diet containing some sucrose. The findings are preclinical and point to diet balance rather than simple sugar elimination.

articles.title: Can fasting fight gum disease? Scientists find surprising link
articles.summary_3_sentences: A small clinical study found that a low-calorie fasting-style diet reduced inflammation linked to gum disease. The findings suggest diet may influence periodontal health alongside oral hygiene.

articles.title: Tea can improve your health and longevity, but the way you drink it matters
articles.summary_3_sentences: A review linked tea consumption with potential benefits for cardiovascular disease, diabetes, cancer, cognitive decline, and muscle loss. The article cautions that bottled and bubble teas may contain ingredients that reduce health value.

articles.title: Ancient Chinese medicine could transform hair loss treatment
articles.summary_3_sentences: Research on Polygonum multiflorum suggests possible mechanisms for hair-growth effects, including hormone, follicle, signaling, and blood-flow pathways. The article links traditional use with modern hair biology.

articles.title: Traditional Chinese Herb Shows Promise for Hair Loss
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article reports that Polygonum multiflorum may be useful for male and female pattern hair loss. Details are limited, and the summary indicates early or exploratory promise.

articles.title: Vitamin B12 and folate deficiencies linked to chronic fatigue
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers in Japan linked low vitamin B12 and folate levels with fatigue and low motivation in otherwise healthy people. The findings suggest vitamin status may contribute to chronic tiredness.

articles.title: Scientists discover hidden gut-brain circuit that triggers protein cravings
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers found that protein shortage can trigger gut-to-brain signals that shift animals toward seeking essential amino acids instead of sugar. The study may inform appetite and nutrition research.

articles.title: Scientists say guava juice could make iron supplements work better
articles.summary_3_sentences: A review of 17 studies found that guava juice, especially with iron supplements, was linked to improved hemoglobin levels in women and teenage girls. The vitamin C-rich juice may support iron absorption in anemia prevention efforts.

articles.title: Surprising research reveals why you shouldn't add bananas to your smoothies
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers found that adding bananas to berry smoothies can reduce absorption of flavanols. The study shows that food combinations can affect how much nutrition is bioavailable.

articles.title: Scientists discover the perfect temperature to keep mangoes fresh much longer
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers found that storing mangoes at 54°F slowed ripening, preserved firmness and moisture, and helped retain antioxidants. The study also examined internal defense systems activated by cold storage.

articles.title: Maternal weight may influence microbiota signaling in amniotic fluid
articles.summary_3_sentences: A University of Oulu study suggests maternal weight-related factors are reflected in microbiota-derived signaling particles in both gut and amniotic fluid. The findings suggest microbiota signaling may extend into the fetal environment.

articles.title: Glucosamine Consumption is Linked to Increased Dementia Risk
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article reports an association between glucosamine consumption and increased dementia risk based on health-record analysis. It raises concerns about supplement use but does not prove causality.

articles.title: A Gut Microbe Can Modulate the Severity of Sepsis
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article describes research suggesting a gut microbe can influence sepsis severity through immune regulation. It proposes targeting microbes as a possible way to modulate immune responses in sepsis.

articles.title: This common amino acid helped mice survive deadly inflammation
articles.summary_3_sentences: In mice, methionine improved survival during severe infections and inflammatory conditions by boosting kidney filtration of inflammatory molecules. The study is preclinical and does not establish human benefit.

articles.title: This tomato-soy juice reduced inflammation in just four weeks
articles.summary_3_sentences: A clinical study found that adults with obesity who drank a tomato-soy juice daily for four weeks had reductions in several inflammatory proteins. A control tomato juice did not produce the same effect.

articles.title: New MIND Tool Analyzes Microbiomes to Understand & Alter Them
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article introduces a MIND tool for analyzing gut microbiomes and linking microbial communities to disease. It may help researchers understand and potentially modify microbiome composition.

articles.title: The mystery of the eye disease that can blind infants
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article discusses retinopathy of prematurity, an infant eye disease that can cause permanent blindness if untreated. It highlights unexplained variation in treatment rates between Norwegian hospitals.

articles.title: How co-parenting reduces overall parental stress
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article discusses research and practice findings on how co-parenting and support networks can reduce parental stress. It emphasizes the value of a broader caregiving community.

articles.title: Fragile X deficits in mice respond to gene therapy
articles.summary_3_sentences: A gene therapy replacing a missing brain protein restored brain activity and improved behavior in a mouse model of fragile X syndrome. The findings are preclinical and suggest a possible future disease-targeting approach.

articles.title: Later menarche may signal childhood factors linked to adult health risks
articles.summary_3_sentences: Research presented at ENDO 2026 linked later menarche with childhood influences associated with several adult health conditions. The reported associations included tobacco use disorder and digestive, heart, bladder, joint, and brain conditions.

articles.title: Common plastic chemical linked to lifelong anxiety in new study
articles.summary_3_sentences: Male rats exposed to DEHP before and shortly after birth showed increased anxiety-like behavior as adults. The study is animal research and does not establish direct human effects.

articles.title: Brain scans reveal two distinct types of autism
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers combined brain scans from nearly 1,000 autistic people with mouse model data and identified two possible biological autism subtypes. The subtypes involved higher-than-usual and lower-than-usual brain connectivity patterns.

articles.title: Scientists discover gut bacteria that may help protect against autism and ADHD
articles.summary_3_sentences: A study linked epigenetic changes at birth, infant gut microbiome development, and early signs of autism and ADHD by age three. The article suggests prenatal gene-microbe interactions may influence early brain development.

articles.title: CT tissue images can now be virtually stained in 3D
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers developed a method to virtually stain CT tissue images in 3D. The technology could complement traditional pathology, which relies on thin stained tissue sections.

articles.title: First new US sunscreen ingredient since 1999 approved by FDA. A skin scientist explains how bemotrizinol works
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article reports FDA approval of bemotrizinol, the first new U.S. sunscreen ingredient since 1999. It explains the development in the context of summer sun protection.

articles.title: Think human anatomy is finished? Scientists say think again
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article notes that scientists continue to identify new anatomical details and overlooked structures in the human body. It emphasizes individual variation and the continuing development of anatomy research.

articles.title: A classic brain test exposed AI's biggest weakness
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers tested AI models with a classic psychology attention task and found performance degraded sharply as task length and complexity increased. Some systems fell from high accuracy to near failure.

articles.title: Long-lived families reveal a rare genetic clue to healthy aging
articles.summary_3_sentences: A study of long-lived families identified rare variants that may support healthier aging. One mutation appeared to reduce inflammation, potentially delaying disease and extending healthy years.

articles.title: Humans may have hidden regenerative powers
articles.summary_3_sentences: In animal studies, a two-stage treatment redirected healing away from scarring and toward regrowth after amputation. The approach restored bone, joints, ligaments, and tendons in preclinical work.

articles.title: Scientists discover inherited traits that break Mendel's Laws of genetics
articles.summary_3_sentences: A mouse study found inherited epigenetic traits that do not follow classic Mendelian patterns. Researchers identified hundreds of unexpected DNA-mark behaviors and a naturally occurring mammalian paramutation.

articles.title: Scientists discover ancient single-celled ancestors still live on in your blood
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers reconstructed the evolutionary history of blood cells and found evidence linking them to single-celled ancestors from about 700 million years ago. The study traces the origins of modern immune functions.

articles.title: Popular anti-aging drug combo caused severe brain damage in mice
articles.summary_3_sentences: A mouse study found that an anti-aging drug combination caused severe myelin loss and brain changes resembling chemotherapy-related cognitive effects. The findings are preclinical and raise safety questions for the drug combination.

articles.title: How woodchips can help keep ticks off trails
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article discusses using woodchips as a practical way to reduce tick exposure on trails. It is framed around outdoor activity during warmer months.

articles.title: DNA time stamps reveal the strawberry's surprising origins
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers used genetic traces from transposable elements to reconstruct the complex evolutionary history of strawberries. The findings show modern strawberries arose through multiple ancient genome-merging events.

articles.title: Beneath our feet lies a fungal superhighway stretching 68 quadrillion miles
articles.summary_3_sentences: Scientists mapped vast underground fungal networks and estimated they move large amounts of carbon dioxide into soils each year. The networks support plants and may influence climate regulation.

articles.title: Millipedes beat vertebrates to land by 80 million years
articles.summary_3_sentences: A study completing the millipede evolutionary family tree suggests they were on land nearly 460 million years ago. The findings also address early chemical defenses in these ancient arthropods.

articles.title: A legendary golden fabric lost for 2,000 years has returned
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers in South Korea recreated sea silk using fibers from a clam and found its golden shine comes from protein structures rather than pigment. The structural color may remain vibrant for centuries.

articles.title: These tiny holes could change how the world cleans water
articles.summary_3_sentences: A nature-inspired membrane with uniform one-nanometer pores can separate molecules with high precision. The technology may reduce energy use and improve water reuse in industries such as pharmaceuticals and textiles.

articles.title: Scientists propose a radical new theory for how life began on Earth
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers propose that mineral nanoparticles acted as natural catalysts and energy processors in early Earth chemistry. These "nanozymes" may help explain the transition from nonliving chemistry to early biological systems.

articles.title: Beluga whales keep switching mates and it may be saving their species
articles.summary_3_sentences: DNA analysis of more than 600 belugas in Alaska's Bristol Bay found that both males and females often have offspring with different partners over time. The flexible mating system may support genetic diversity.

articles.title: A hidden pollutant is changing how the world's forests breathe
articles.summary_3_sentences: A global analysis found nitrogen pollution can either speed or slow forest soil respiration depending on ecosystem conditions. The results reveal tipping points relevant to carbon storage and climate resilience.

articles.title: The ocean's health may depend on a tiny microbe inside fish
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers found evidence that gut bacteria in marine fish help produce calcium carbonate, a mineral involved in ocean chemistry and carbon storage. The findings revise the idea that fish perform this process alone.

articles.title: The secret to pigeons' incredible navigation was hiding in their liver
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers identified iron-filled immune cells in pigeon livers that may act as magnetic sensors. Birds lacking these cells had trouble navigating under overcast skies.

articles.title: This newly discovered raptor may have hunted like a giant heron
articles.summary_3_sentences: A newly described Patagonian dinosaur, Kank australis, may have hunted fish using a long flexible neck like a heron. The finding changes views of predator diversity in its ecosystem.

articles.title: Scientists say evolution may work differently than we thought
articles.summary_3_sentences: A University of Michigan study challenges the idea that most fixed genetic changes are neutral. Researchers found beneficial mutations may be common but often fail to spread because environments keep changing.

articles.title: DNA solves 250-year-old mystery of the Seychelles' lost crocodiles
articles.summary_3_sentences: DNA from museum specimens showed the extinct Seychelles crocodiles were an isolated population of saltwater crocodiles rather than a unique species. The population likely reached the islands by long-distance ocean dispersal.

articles.title: How a 45‑minute family exercise session could boost thinking skills
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article discusses research suggesting a 45-minute family exercise session may improve thinking skills. It is framed around low physical activity levels among adults and adolescents.

articles.title: World Cup: What's just the right height for a soccer player?
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article discusses height and selection pressures in soccer performance. It frames sports as a context where physical traits can influence opportunity and role.

articles.title: 90-120 Minutes of Strength Training Per Week Increases Lifespan
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article reports that 90 to 120 minutes of weekly resistance training was associated with longer lifespan. It describes a dose-response relationship between strength-training duration and longevity.

articles.title: Scientists found the strength training sweet spot for a longer life
articles.summary_3_sentences: A 30-year study of more than 147,000 people linked 90 to 120 minutes of weekly strength training with lower mortality, especially from cardiovascular and neurological diseases. Combining strength and aerobic exercise showed stronger benefits.

articles.title: A 100-year-old piano mystery has finally been solved
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers used high-speed sensors to show that elite pianists can subtly manipulate keys in ways that affect perceived tone color. The study addresses a long-standing debate in music performance.

articles.title: Study Examines the Genetic Link Between Cannabis Use and Psychosis
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article describes a study in Biological Psychiatry Global Open Science on genetic links between cannabis use disorder and psychosis. Details are limited, but it focuses on biological mechanisms connecting cannabis use and psychiatric risk.

articles.title: Study Found Minimal Impact of Cannabis Use on Male Fertility
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article reports research on cannabis use and the male hormonal system, with findings described as showing minimal impact on male fertility. The summary provides limited study detail.

articles.title: This giant tropical fruit could help reverse gum disease damage
articles.summary_3_sentences: A biomaterial made from jackfruit latex, pomegranate peel, and simvastatin showed early promise for treating severe gum disease. Early tests suggest effects on infection, inflammation, and tissue or bone rebuilding.

articles.title: Scientists are seriously asking if bees and ChatGPT are conscious
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article discusses research on consciousness in insects and AI systems, arguing that behavior alone is insufficient evidence. Researchers focus on internal mechanisms and currently consider today's AI unlikely to be conscious.

excluded_items.title: From Atlas to Action: Going Deeper in the Next Era of Spatial Biology
